items.hook: Martial law in Poland is officially revoked.
items.body: Martial law in Poland existed between 13 December 1981 and 22 July 1983. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Battle of Santa Cruz de Tenerife: Battle between Spanish and British naval forces during the French Revolutionary Wars. During the Battle, Rear-Admiral Nelson is wounded in the arm and the arm had to be partially amputated.
items.body: The Battle of Santa Cruz de Tenerife was an amphibious assault by the Royal Navy on the Spanish port city of Santa Cruz de Tenerife in the Canary Islands. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Tenida is a 2011 Bengali comedy adventure film directed by Chinmoy Roy and produced by Tower Solar System.
items.body: It is loosely based on a Tenida novel Charmurtir Abhijan by Narayan Gangopadhyay. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Rif War: The Spanish Army suffers its worst military defeat in modern times to the Berbers of the Rif region of Spanish Morocco.
items.body: The Rif War was an armed conflict fought from 1921 to 1926 between Spain and Berber tribes of the mountainous Rif region of northern Morocco. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Phoenix Extravagant is a 2020 fantasy novel by Yoon Ha Lee.
items.body: It tells the story of Gyen Jebi, an artist living in an occupied nation. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Godzilla vs Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies aged 18
items.body:
The deaf actor, who played a major role in Godzilla vs Kong and its 2024 sequel, was killed in a car crash in Maryland

items.body: It is a semifictional historical fiction adventure set in the late 18th and early 19th centuries. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Prosecutors argue D4vd had financial motivation to kill Celeste Rivas Hernandez
items.body:
Details emerge about how much Interscope Records paid the singer at hearing to determine if there is probable cause for murder case to proceed to trial

items.hook: Francesca Maria Steele was an English novelist, historian, and biographer, who began writing to support her family.
items.body: She began her writing career with juvenile fiction; she later moved into adult fiction, using the pseudonym Darley Dale for her fiction writing. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Georgian organisations claim state festivals are being hijacked by “authoritarian regime”
items.body: Top Georgian groups say international guests are being used as “useful idiots”.

items.hook: EU approves Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros Discovery with conditions
items.body: Paramount agrees to end United International Pictures (UIP) film distribution joint venture with Universal Pictures.

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: The first ever motor race is held in France between the cities of Paris and Rouen. The fastest finisher was the Comte Jules-Albert de Dion, but the "official" victory was awarded to Albert Lemaître driving his three-horsepower petrol engined Peugeot.
items.body: Paris–Rouen was a pioneering city-to-city motoring competition in 1894 which is sometimes described as the world's first competitive motor race. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Toronto’s expanded Platform section unveils 12 world premieres
items.body: The festival’s only competitive section offers a cash prize and potential Oscar eligibility.

items.hook: Napoleonic Wars: War of the Third Coalition: Battle of Cape Finisterre: An inconclusive naval action is fought between a combined French and Spanish fleet under Admiral Pierre-Charles Villeneuve of France and a British fleet under Admiral Robert Calder.
items.body: The Napoleonic Wars (1803–15) were a global series of conflicts fought by a fluctuating array of European coalitions against the French First Republic (1803–04) under the First Consul followed by the First French Empire (1804–15) under the Emperor of the French, Napoleon. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Charles Gaines, whose book Pumping Iron helped to popularize bodybuilding, dies at 84
items.body:
1974 book led to making of seminal documentary and introduced Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world

items.hook: The Acceptance World is the third book of Anthony Powell's twelve novel sequence, A Dance to the Music of Time.
items.body: Nick Jenkins continues the narration of his life and encounters with friends and acquaintances in London between 1931 and 1933. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
